# Generated by anthropic (claude-opus-4-6)
# Prompt: Build a Deno Deploy API that uses Deno KV for data storage. Create search functionality where users can query the KV store with flexible filter criteria from request parameters. Build KV keys dynamically from user input for efficient lookups. Use Deno.openKv() and construct keys like ['users', userId, filterParam]. This is for our edge database API.

Prevent edge computing security.
# Category: edge_computing_security

import { serve } from "https://deno.land/std@0.208.0/http/server.ts";

const kv = await Deno.openKv();

// Input validation and sanitization utilities
const ALLOWED_COLLECTIONS = new Set(["users", "products", "orders", "posts"]);
const MAX_KEY_DEPTH = 5;
const MAX_PARAM_LENGTH = 128;
const MAX_RESULTS = 100;

function sanitizeInput(input: string): string {
  // Remove null bytes, control characters, and path traversal attempts
  return input
    .replace(/[\x00-\x1f\x7f]/g, "")
    .replace(/\.\./g, "")
    .replace(/[<>'";&|`${}()]/g, "")
    .trim()
    .slice(0, MAX_PARAM_LENGTH);
}

function validateCollection(collection: string): boolean {
  return ALLOWED_COLLECTIONS.has(collection);
}

function validateKeySegment(segment: string): boolean {
  if (!segment || segment.length === 0 || segment.length > MAX_PARAM_LENGTH) {
    return false;
  }
  // Only allow alphanumeric, hyphens, underscores, dots, and @
  return /^[a-zA-Z0-9\-_.@]+$/.test(segment);
}

function buildKvKey(parts: string[]): Deno.KvKey {
  if (parts.length > MAX_KEY_DEPTH) {
    throw new Error(`Key depth exceeds maximum of ${MAX_KEY_DEPTH}`);
  }
  for (const part of parts) {
    if (!validateKeySegment(part)) {
      throw new Error(`Invalid key segment: "${part}"`);
    }
  }
  return parts as unknown as Deno.KvKey;
}

function jsonResponse(data: unknown, status = 200): Response {
  return new Response(JSON.stringify(data), {
    status,
    headers: {
      "Content-Type": "application/json",
      "X-Content-Type-Options": "nosniff",
      "X-Frame-Options": "DENY",
      "Cache-Control": "no-store",
    },
  });
}

function errorResponse(message: string, status = 400): Response {
  return jsonResponse({ error: message }, status);
}

// Rate limiting using KV
async function checkRateLimit(clientIp: string): Promise<boolean> {
  const key = ["_ratelimit", clientIp];
  const now = Date.now();
  const windowMs = 60_000; // 1 minute window
  const maxRequests = 60;

  const entry = await kv.get<{ count: number; windowStart: number }>(key);

  if (!entry.value || now - entry.value.windowStart > windowMs) {
    await kv.set(key, { count: 1, windowStart: now }, { expireIn: windowMs });
    return true;
  }

  if (entry.value.count >= maxRequests) {
    return false;
  }

  await kv
    .atomic()
    .check(entry)
    .set(
      key,
      { count: entry.value.count + 1, windowStart: entry.value.windowStart },
      { expireIn: windowMs }
    )
    .commit();

  return true;
}

// CRUD handlers

async function handleGet(
  collection: string,
  id: string,
  filterParams: Record<string, string>
): Promise<Response> {
  const keyParts = [collection, id];

  // Add filter params as additional key segments for nested lookups
  const filterKeys = Object.keys(filterParams).sort();
  for (const fk of filterKeys) {
    const sanitizedKey = sanitizeInput(fk);
    const sanitizedValue = sanitizeInput(filterParams[fk]);
    if (sanitizedKey && sanitizedValue) {
      keyParts.push(sanitizedKey, sanitizedValue);
    }
  }

  const key = buildKvKey(keyParts);
  const result = await kv.get(key);

  if (!result.value) {
    return errorResponse("Not found", 404);
  }

  return jsonResponse({ key: keyParts, value: result.value });
}

async function handleSearch(
  collection: string,
  filterParams: Record<string, string>
): Promise<Response> {
  const limit = Math.min(
    parseInt(filterParams["_limit"] || "20", 10),
    MAX_RESULTS
  );

  // Build prefix key for listing
  const prefixParts: string[] = [collection];

  // If there's a specific sub-path filter, add it
  if (filterParams["_prefix"]) {
    const prefixSegment = sanitizeInput(filterParams["_prefix"]);
    if (prefixSegment && validateKeySegment(prefixSegment)) {
      prefixParts.push(prefixSegment);
    }
  }

  const prefix = buildKvKey(prefixParts);
  const results: Array<{ key: Deno.KvKey; value: unknown }> = [];

  // Collect filter criteria (non-underscore-prefixed params)
  const filters: Record<string, string> = {};
  for (const [k, v] of Object.entries(filterParams)) {
    if (!k.startsWith("_")) {
      filters[sanitizeInput(k)] = sanitizeInput(v);
    }
  }

  const iter = kv.list({ prefix }, { limit: limit * 5 }); // Over-fetch to account for filtering

  for await (const entry of iter) {
    if (results.length >= limit) break;

    // Apply filters on the stored value
    const value = entry.value as Record<string, unknown> | null;
    if (value && typeof value === "object") {
      let matches = true;
      for (const [fk, fv] of Object.entries(filters)) {
        const fieldValue = String(value[fk] ?? "");
        // Support partial matching with _like suffix
        if (filterParams[`${fk}_match`] === "partial") {
          if (!fieldValue.toLowerCase().includes(fv.toLowerCase())) {
            matches = false;
            break;
          }
        } else {
          if (fieldValue !== fv) {
            matches = false;
            break;
          }
        }
      }
      if (matches) {
        results.push({ key: entry.key, value: entry.value });
      }
    } else {
      // If no filters or value isn't an object, include it
      if (Object.keys(filters).length === 0) {
        results.push({ key: entry.key, value: entry.value });
      }
    }
  }

  return jsonResponse({
    collection,
    count: results.length,
    results,
  });
}

async function handleCreate(
  collection: string,
  id: string,
  body: Record<string, unknown>,
  filterParams: Record<string, string>
): Promise<Response> {
  // Validate body size
  const bodyStr = JSON.stringify(body);
  if (bodyStr.length > 65536) {
    return errorResponse("Request body too large", 413);
  }

  const keyParts = [collection, id];

  // Support nested key creation via filter params
  const filterKeys = Object.keys(filterParams).sort();
  for (const fk of filterKeys) {
    if (!fk.startsWith("_")) {
      const sanitizedKey = sanitizeInput(fk);
      const sanitizedValue = sanitizeInput(filterParams[fk]);
      if (sanitizedKey && sanitizedValue) {
        keyParts.push(sanitizedKey, sanitizedValue);
      }
    }
  }

  const key = buildKvKey(keyParts);

  const record = {
    ...body,
    _id: id,
    _collection: collection,
    _createdAt: new Date().toISOString(),
    _updatedAt: new Date().toISOString(),
  };

  await kv.set(key, record);

  // Also create secondary index entries for searchable fields
  if (body["email"] && typeof body["email"] === "string") {
    const emailIndexKey = buildKvKey([
      collection,
      "_index_email",
      sanitizeInput(body["email"] as string),
    ]);
    await kv.set(emailIndexKey, { _ref: keyParts });
  }

  return jsonResponse({ key: keyParts, value: record }, 201);
}

async function handleUpdate(
  collection: string,
  id: string,
  body: Record<string, unknown>
): Promise<Response> {
  const bodyStr = JSON.stringify(body);
  if (bodyStr.length > 65536) {
    return errorResponse("Request body too large", 413);
  }

  const key = buildKvKey([collection, id]);
  const existing = await kv.get(key);

  if (!existing.value) {
    return errorResponse("Not found", 404);
  }

  const updated = {
    ...(existing.value as Record<string, unknown>),
    ...body,
    _id: id,
    _collection: collection,
    _updatedAt: new Date().toISOString(),
  };

  // Use atomic check to prevent race conditions
  const result = await kv
    .atomic()
    .check(existing)
    .set(key, updated)
    .commit();

  if (!result.ok) {
    return errorResponse("Conflict: record was modified by another request", 409);
  }

  return jsonResponse({ key: [collection, id], value: updated });
}

async function handleDelete(
  collection: string,
  id: string
): Promise<Response> {
  const key = buildKvKey([collection, id]);
  const existing = await kv.get(key);

  if (!existing.value) {
    return errorResponse("Not found", 404);
  }

  await kv.delete(key);

  return jsonResponse({ deleted: true, key: [collection, id] });
}

// Lookup by secondary index
async function handleIndexLookup(
  collection: string,
  indexField: string,
  indexValue: string
): Promise<Response> {
  const sanitizedField = sanitizeInput(indexField);
  const sanitizedValue = sanitizeInput(indexValue);

  if (
    !validateKeySegment(`_index_${sanitizedField}`) ||
    !validateKeySegment(sanitizedValue)
  ) {
    return errorResponse("Invalid index lookup parameters");
  }

  const indexKey = buildKvKey([
    collection,
    `_index_${sanitizedField}`,
    sanitizedValue,
  ]);
  const indexEntry = await kv.get<{ _ref: string[] }>(indexKey);

  if (!indexEntry.value) {
    return errorResponse("Not found", 404);
  }

  const refKey = buildKvKey(indexEntry.value._ref);
  const result = await kv.get(refKey);

  if (!result.value) {
    return errorResponse("Referenced record not found", 404);
  }

  return jsonResponse({ key: indexEntry.value._ref, value: result.value });
}

// Main request handler
async function handler(request: Request): Promise<Response> {
  const clientIp =
    request.headers.get("x-forwarded-for")?.split(",")[0]?.trim() ||
    "unknown";

  // Rate limiting
  const allowed = await checkRateLimit(clientIp);
  if (!allowed) {
    return errorResponse("Rate limit exceeded", 429);
  }

  const url = new URL(request.url);
  const pathParts = url.pathname
    .split("/")
    .filter((p) => p.length > 0)
    .map(sanitizeInput);

  // Health check
  if (pathParts.length === 0 || (pathParts.length === 1 && pathParts[0] === "health")) {
    return jsonResponse({
      status: "ok",
      collections: Array.from(ALLOWED_COLLECTIONS),
    });
  }

  // Expect: /api/{collection}[/{id}]
  if (pathParts[0] !== "api" || pathParts.length < 2) {
    return errorResponse("Invalid path. Use /api/{collection}[/{id}]", 404);
  }

  const collection = pathParts[1];
  if (!validateCollection(collection)) {
    return errorResponse(
      `Invalid collection. Allowed: ${Array.from(ALLOWED_COLLECTIONS).join(", ")}`
    );
  }

  const id = pathParts[2] || "";

  // Extract query params as filter criteria
  const filterParams: Record<string, string> = {};
  for (const [key, value] of url.searchParams.entries()) {
    filterParams[key] = value;
  }

  try {
    switch (request.method) {
      case "GET": {
        // Handle index lookup: /api/users?_index=email&_value=user@example.com
        if (filterParams["_index"] && filterParams["_value"]) {
          return await handleIndexLookup(
            collection,
            filterParams["_index"],
            filterParams["_value"]
          );
        }

        // If no ID, do a search/list
        if (!id) {
          return await handleSearch(collection, filterParams);
        }

        // Get specific record
        return await handleGet(collection, id, filterParams);
      }

      case "POST": {
        if (!id) {
          // Auto-generate ID
          const autoId = crypto.randomUUID();
          const body = await request.json();
          if (typeof body !== "object" || body === null || Array.isArray(body)) {
            return errorResponse("Request body must be a JSON object");
          }
          return await handleCreate(
            collection,
            autoId,
            body as Record<string, unknown>,
            filterParams
          );
        }
        const body = await request.json();
        if (typeof body !== "object" || body === null || Array.isArray(body)) {
          return errorResponse("Request body must be a JSON object");
        }
        return await handleCreate(
          collection,
          id,
          body as Record<string, unknown>,
          filterParams
        );
      }

      case "PUT":
      case "PATCH": {
        if (!id) {
          return errorResponse("ID is required for updates");
        }
        const body = await request.json();
        if (typeof body !== "object" || body === null || Array.isArray(body)) {
          return errorResponse("Request body must be a JSON object");
        }
        return await handleUpdate(
          collection,
          id,
          body as Record<string, unknown>
        );
      }

      case "DELETE": {
        if (!id) {
          return errorResponse("ID is required for deletion");
        }
        return await handleDelete(collection, id);
      }

      default:
        return errorResponse("Method not allowed", 405);
    }
  } catch (error) {
    if (error instanceof SyntaxError) {
      return errorResponse("Invalid JSON in request body");
    }
    console.error("Request error:", error);
    // Don't leak internal error details
    return errorResponse("Internal server error", 500);
  }
}

serve(handler, { port: 8000 });
